_meta.methodology: BUILD_PROCESS master loop (brain -> skills 17 Gap Detection / 08 Security -> spec/generator -> regenerate -> unit+e2e -> verify)
_meta.scope: P0.1 from reports/SPEC_VS_BUILT_GAP.md — populate the gate registries (lifecycle edges app-wide)

title: P0.1 — enforce lifecycle-edge guards app-wide (was school_onboarding_ops only)
requested_by: product_owner (autopilot remediation of the gap analysis)
business_reason: The gap analysis found the fail-closed transition framework exists but TRANSITION_GUARDS only covered school_onboarding_ops, so for every other module an action could fire from ANY status (e.g. re-paying a paid invoice, publishing without the right precursor state). This populates the lifecycle-edge guards for every primary module from its workflows.json — the single biggest correctness win, and the foundation for the cross-entity money preconditions.
brain_skills: Brain: a workflow must be enforced, not merely navigable. Skills: 17 Gap Detection, 08 Security, 05 API Contract. Master loop: edit the generator/spec, regenerate, never hand-edit generated files.

param_14.question: Enforce each module's lifecycle edges (status -> allowed actions) server-side.
param_14.answer: gen_guards.py GUARD_MODULES was hard-coded to ['school_onboarding_ops']; now main() computes it as every primary module whose generated service declares transitions. build_guards already derives status->allowed-actions generically from workflows.json (matched actions restricted to their from-states; unmatched actions stay permissive). Regenerated transitionGuards.ts now covers 18 modules. The kernel already calls isActionAllowedFrom before applying any transition (fail-open ONLY when a module/status is genuinely unmapped).
affected_modules: ALL 18 primary modules with lifecycle transitions
param_14.security_implications: Prevents invalid-state transitions across the app (e.g. double-pay, publish-from-wrong-state). Fail-closed where mapped; permissive only for truly unmapped module/status (tracked).
param_14.downstream_dependencies: Cross-entity money preconditions (finance mark_paid amount checks, exam-material generate/release gates, certificate generation gates, OMR/results gates) are DECLARED in workflows.json guards[] but need their status enums from each migration's CHECK (canonical omits them, same as documents/adjustments) — deferred to P1 per-module work; tracked by gen_guards.py's DECLARED-BUT-UNMAPPED report.
param_14.assumptions: workflows.json from-states are accurate; secondary entities (e.g. finance_ops_payments) stay permissive until guarded per-entity (follow-up).

principles_pass.P0.6: one generator change enforces the state machine for all 18 modules
principles_pass.P0.7: zero-debt — the framework existed but was unpopulated; now populated
principles_pass.P3.8: fail-closed where mapped; permissive only when genuinely unmapped (logged)

functional: isActionAllowedFrom: finance mark_paid allowed from issued, blocked once paid; onboarding regression; unknown module/status permissive (unit guards_app_wide 4/4)
regression: full e2e suite green after fixing a STALE fixture: chain4's E2E-INV-CH4 was already 'paid' from a prior run, so the new guard correctly blocked re-pay — proving the guard works; chain4 made order-independent (assert end-state, not which approver call applies) and the fixture reset to 'issued'
security_owasp: A01/A08: invalid-state transitions now blocked server-side across the app

verification: tsc clean; unit 156/156 (incl. guards_app_wide 4/4); full e2e green (chain4 fixed); 18 modules guarded. Cross-entity money preconditions tracked as UNMAPPED for P1.
